post.content:
Hello LeetCode community,

I wanted to share my Uber OA (Online Assessment) experience to give back to this amazing community.

I attempted the OA today. I’m not entirely sure how I got shortlisted, but I’ve been applying to Uber frequently, so maybe persistence paid off! I don't even know which level is it. Below is a breakdown of the problems I encountered. While the exact problem statements, inputs, and outputs might differ slightly, this should give you a clear idea 😇. I will also put some details on my aproaches, so you will get idea what were the accepted solutions. 

---

### Problem 1: Valid String Check
Difficulty: Easy

##### Description:
Given a string, check if it is valid or not. A valid string must:
* Be divisible by 3 (interpreted as the numeric value of the string is divisible by 3).
* Contain the digit '7' at least twice.

##### Testcases:

**Valid strings:** 771, 777, 123777
**Invalid strings:** 12345, 71, 171, 70

I implemented simple approach, do as asked in description for each string.

---

### Problem 2: Command Frequency Counter
Difficulty: Easy

##### Description:
You are given an array of commands. There are only three possible commands: `cmd1`, `cmd2`, and `cmd3`. Your task is to return an array of size 3 that contains the frequency of each command in the input array, in the order [frequency of cmd1, frequency of cmd2, frequency of cmd3].

Also, any element in the form of !\<index> refers to the same command that was present at the <index> position in the input array. Note that indexing starts from 1 (not 0).

##### Input:
```
[cmd1, cmd2, cmd3, !1, !2, cmd3, cmd1]
```

##### Output:
```
[3, 2, 2]
```

At index 4 (!1), the command is the same as the command at index 1 (cmd1).
At index 5 (!2), the command is the same as the command at index 2 (cmd2).

I solved in one pass with not extra space.

---

### Problem 3: Regional Maximum Finder
Difficulty: Medium or maybe Hard

##### Description:
You are given a 2D array. Your task is to find the regional maxima in the array and return a 2D array of size (X * 2) where each row contains the position [i, j] of a regional maximum.

**Definition of Regional Maximum:**
A cell (i, j) is considered a regional maximum if:
* array[i][j] != 0
* array[i][j] is the maximum value within its region.

**Definition of Region:**
For a cell (i, j) with value cell:
* The region is defined as the rectangular area (`i - cell` to `i + cell`) * (`j - cell` to `j + cell`).
* Exclude the corner cells from above: `(i - cell, j - cell)`, `(i - cell, j + cell)`, `(i + cell, j - cell)` and `(i + cell, j + cell)`.
* If the calculated region goes out of bounds, ignore those out-of-bound cells.

##### Input:
```
[
 [3, 0, 1],
 [2, 0, 0],
 [0, 0, 0],
]
```
##### Output:
```
[[0, 0], [0, 2]]
```

The dimensions of the array were relatively small, like 100 * 100, so performance constraints were manageable.
I solved with worse time-complexity(4 nested loops) same as what comes to mind at first glance, not sure how to optimise. Would love to know your thoughts

---

### Problem 4: Sliding Window Problem

Difficulty: Leetcode says Hard

The fourth problem was based on this LeetCode question:
[Longest Continuous Subarray With Absolute Diff Less Than or Equal to Limit](https://leetcode.com/problems/longest-continuous-subarray-with-absolute-diff-less-than-or-equal-to-limit/)

The problem statement was phrased differently, but the solution required implementing the exact same logic as this LeetCode problem.

I gave the most optimal solution, you can check out discussion tab of the problem.

---

I was able to submit solutions for all with 7 minutes remaining. Each problem had score of 300.
This is my experience so far! I’ll update this post with more details if the process moves forward. Hope this helps someone preparing for their Uber OA!

Good luck to everyone!

Problem 3 has a sparse table solution I think although I've never done one in 2D. Would be tough in an OA but a solution is possible given enough time.

edit: in total the algo I propose is `O(R*C*log(R)*log(C))` time and space. Answering all the queries, `O(R*C)`, involves 8 (yikes) `O(1)` lookups in the 2D sparse table.

edit 2: I'd only attempt this personally in an OA if I had a lot of extra time and I got TLE with brute force, otherwise probably not worth the risk!

A sparse table is a structure for computing any idempotent operation like max over a range. It's similar to binary lifting. In a 1D sparse table, for each entry `i`, we record the max over `i..i+2**p-1`. Any interval we want can be written as the union of two overlapping smaller intervals with power-of-two length we've precomputed.

In a 2D sparse table I imagine we'd need `table[i][j][pr][pc]` to look up arbitrary rectangular regions. To fill in the table we would
* `pr==0`: single row, same fill process as a 1D sparse table for all the `pc` entries
* `pr==1`: same idea, first set `table[i][j][1][0]` then set all the larger `pc` entries
* and so on, first step is setting `table[i][j][pr][0]` then setting larger column ranges

This produces a 2D sparse table in `O(R*C*log(R)*log(C))` time.

Then we can get the maximum over an arbitrary `2cell x 2cell` region except the corners as follows:
* find the largest power of 2 not larger than cell-1
* use the sparse table to find the max of power-of-two sized regions that touch the four corners of the `2*(cell-1) x 2*(cell-1)` region
  * this way we get the max of everything not in the first and last row and column of the region, thus avoiding the corners
  * using the largest power of two and the four corners (2D analogy of the 1D sparse table logic) makes sure the max includes all interior elements
* then find the max over the first and last row and column of the region except for the four corner cells (yikes)

Finally be careful about edge cases if the corners are out of bounds to avoid index errors in the sparse table. Might not be bad though, the table entries for all nonexistent cells can be set to 0, and maxes over those cells will remain zero as well. So looking up the max for a nonsensical range that goes OOB will either be 0 or the max of the subset of that range that is in bounds.
